item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My favorite kind of articles are those that have some surreal or nonsensical basis, but are actually taken seriously by the author. This is one of those, and is well-written to boot. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But fusing two flesh creatures into a new being is quite a sarkic thing to do…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has got to be one of the better and more bizarre humanoid articles I've seen. Duly granted +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the society you built for this species, as well as the way their geographical differences made each of the three tribes have different histories. The "children's dictatorship" was a cool idea, though I can't help but feel the Church of the Broken God wouldn't mind the metamorphosis-based reproduction of the SCP, considering it's basically making two separate entities whole via assembly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something about those half-people and their conjoining really creeped me out. Job done +1
The various cultures and their relationship with other GoI really filled out the piece without being a trek too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It reminds me of Azimov's The God Themselves, but the djinn part is really interesting.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this - I think it's actually a really good example of how to translate mythological and folkloric material into an SCP article well. It doesn't lean exclusively on the weirdness of the myth, and makes an effort to actively integrate them into the Foundation's world rather than completely isolate and decontextualise them, which is sadly the route a lot of similar articles have taken. I particularly like the stuff about the children's dictatorship, the Cogwork Church's theological involvement, and the lack of data on SCP-3341-C. +1

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
I accept the crosslink to the CotBG hub.

As for the Coldest War crosslink, I am slightly mixed… but I will go with it (since the image of communist uprisings in paranormal communities may feel tangentially inline with the canon).

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Neat. In addition to a few existing links present in the article, mentions to Gru-P and CotBG have also been made. I noticed that the author linked to ORIA's hub, so a link to the CotBG Hub and maybe The Coldest War (closest thing Gru-P has to a GoI hub atm) would not be so out of place. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

While the Foundation has contact with SCP-3341-B since 1953 CE, contact was lost following the fall of the Ethiopian Empire in 1974 CE. Foundation assets were expelled, and GRU Division "P" (GRU-P) was invited to study SCP-3341-B instead, supposedly as part of an experiment to induct non-human sapient species to communism. This lasted until 1993 CE, wherein the Foundation re-established a presence in the Horn of Africa.

To this:

While the Foundation has contact with SCP-3341-B since 1953 CE, contact was lost following the fall of the Ethiopian Empire in 1974 CE. Foundation assets were expelled, and GRU Division "P" (GRU-P) was invited to study SCP-3341-B instead, supposedly as part of an experiment to induct non-human sapient species to communism. This lasted until 1993 CE, wherein the Foundation re-established a presence in the Horn of Africa.

Changing this:

SCP-3341-B1 denotes the Order of Saint-Militant Qaynan, consisting of juvenile SCP-3341 instances affiliated with the Cogwork Orthodox Church. Thus, instead of the class system centered on its life cycle, social hierarchy within SCP-3341-B1 mirrors the Cogwork Orthodox Church.

To this:

SCP-3341-B1 denotes the Order of Saint-Militant Qaynan, consisting of juvenile SCP-3341 instances affiliated with the Cogwork Orthodox Church. Thus, instead of the class system centered on its life cycle, social hierarchy within SCP-3341-B1 mirrors the Cogwork Orthodox Church.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of my favorite interpretations of a mythological creature on this site. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neat. I like the idea here and I feel like you really developed it well. The only thing I'd add is some form of illustration *coughsunnyclockworkthat'syourcuecough*

Jokes aside, it's really well written and a very original idea. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The anomaly presented here is very interesting, and both the metamorphosis and conversion to Church members is very clever. Enthusiastic +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I once saw a similar notion in Hedwig and the Angry Inch, and I thought it was touching and romantic, and now reading the same thing here, it is fucking creepy. Good job.

item.title: Thanks, Attribution, and Other Matters
item.content:encoded:
Allow me to express thanks to a variety of persons including: bittermixin, LordStonefish, Sterbai, DrChandra, among several others.

Image attribution is as follows:

  • Image by Zivya of Wikimedia Commons (https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Shalim.JPG) under CC BY-SA 3.0

References Attribution is as follows:

  • Draft Proposal For Programme Arduinosis 9001 by Roget
  • ORIA Hub by Eskobar and his team for the GoI contest
+ show block
– hide block

SCP-3341 is originally intended to be a meatsuit for the memetic entities called djinn (of ORIA hub), wherein the two instances would have joined to provide a physical medium for the djinn to operate, like a shell. However, their creation made them sapient and the djinn who made them let 3341 to live freely.

And thus, that anomalous species spread out in reasonable numbers.
